summary: My family and I finally made it to Thailand after fleeing from Burma. We lived there for about a year before moving to the United States. Thailand was a place of temporary refuge for us Karen people, where we could rest and rebuild small houses to sleep in after the long and dangerous journey. The weather in Thailand was comfortable, not too hot or cold, which was a relief after all we had been through. Even though we were safe there, many Karen people were still running and searching for a place to call home.

Before coming to Thailand, my family and the Karen people lived in Burma, where life was very difficult. The Burmese soldiers did not like us and wanted us gone, so they burned down our villages and chased us away. I remember seeing houses on fire, hearing gunshots, and people screaming for help as we ran for our lives. It was a dangerous journey through Burma, and many of my people were injured or lost along the way. Burma was the place we had to escape from to find safety and a new home.
